message:
chore(beads): update issue tracking metadata

Sync beads issue state with latest agent work including status
transitions and progress updates across ongoing frankenterm tasks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -833,7 +833,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-2l27x.8","title":"FrankenSQLite migration plan","description":"Plan and prepare for eventual migration from rusqlite to frankensqlite as the storage backend.

## Precondition
frankensqlite (github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensqlite) must complete Phase 5+ (WAL/B-tree/MVCC wired as default execution backend, not just tested components).

## Migration benefits
- MVCC concurrent writers: 50+ agent panes writing simultaneously without single-writer bottleneck
- RaptorQ self-healing WAL: survives torn writes and bit-flips after power loss
- Native mode time-travel: replay any terminal session at any point in time
- XChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption at rest (session state is sensitive)

## Migration tasks (when ready)
1. Add frankensqlite as workspace dependency (it's a 23-crate workspace — evaluate binary size impact)
2. Create storage backend trait to abstract rusqlite vs frankensqlite
3. Implement frankensqlite backend behind feature flag
4. Benchmark: single-writer throughput, concurrent writer throughput, checkpoint latency
5. Migration tool: convert existing rusqlite .db to frankensqlite format
6. Gradual rollout: feature flag default off, test in staging

## Status: DEFERRED until frankensqlite Phase 5+ ships","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":480,"created_at":"2026-02-10T23:20:22.326998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-08T01:05:37.423692Z","defer_until":"2026-06-01T13:00:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["deferred","frankensqlite","migration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2l27x.8","depends_on_id":"ft-2l27x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-2l27x.8","depends_on_id":"ft-2l27x.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-2l27x.9","title":"Graceful shutdown checkpoint & signal handlers","description":"Implement signal handlers that trigger a final checkpoint with type='shutdown' before ft exits, so restarts after clean shutdown don't trigger restore prompts.

## Signal handling
- Register handlers for SIGTERM, SIGINT (Ctrl-C), and SIGHUP
- On signal received:
  1. Log INFO: "Shutdown signal received ({signal}), writing final checkpoint"
  2. Trigger one final checkpoint via the checkpoint engine's event channel
  3. Wait for checkpoint to complete (with 5-second timeout)
  4. Set mux_sessions.shutdown_clean = 1
  5. Log INFO: "Clean shutdown complete, session {id} saved"
  6. Proceed with normal shutdown (stop watcher, close connections)

## Implementation
- Use tokio::signal for async signal handling
- The checkpoint engine (.4) already handles event-triggered checkpoints — send a ShutdownCheckpoint event
- Add a shutdown_tx oneshot channel that the signal handler awaits for confirmation
- If checkpoint times out (5s), log WARN and proceed with shutdown anyway (don't hang)

## ft stop command integration
- ft stop sends SIGTERM to the watcher process
- The watcher's signal handler triggers the shutdown checkpoint
- ft stop waits up to 10 seconds for clean exit, then SIGKILL

## Edge cases
- Double SIGINT (user hits Ctrl-C twice): second signal forces immediate exit without checkpoint
- Signal during an in-progress checkpoint: wait for current checkpoint, skip the shutdown checkpoint (already have recent state)
- Signal before first checkpoint: write a checkpoint even if checkpoint_enabled=false in config

## Unit tests required
- test_shutdown_sets_clean_flag: SIGTERM → shutdown_clean = 1
- test_shutdown_checkpoint_written: verify checkpoint with type='shutdown' in database
- test_shutdown_timeout: slow checkpoint → timeout → still exits cleanly
- test_double_sigint: second signal forces immediate exit
- test_ft_stop_triggers_clean_shutdown: ft stop → watcher exits with shutdown_clean = 1

## Logging
- INFO: "Shutdown signal received ({signal}), writing final checkpoint"
- INFO: "Clean shutdown complete, session {session_id} saved ({pane_count} panes)"
- WARN: "Shutdown checkpoint timed out after 5s, exiting without final save"
- WARN: "Double signal received, forcing immediate exit"
- DEBUG: "Shutdown checkpoint written: checkpoint_id={id}, elapsed={ms}ms"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-02-10T23:45:51.036219Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:26:34.408533Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:26:34.408533Z","close_reason":"shutdown_checkpoint() method in snapshot_engine.rs + main.rs watcher lifecycle wiring complete (30f22fc, 4f83353)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["runtime","shutdown","signals"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2l27x.9","depends_on_id":"ft-2l27x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-2l27x.9","depends_on_id":"ft-2l27x.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-2l7jp","title":"[Subtask ft-e34d9] Route search_bridge mpsc through runtime_compat alias","description":"Expose unbounded_channel in runtime_compat::mpsc (tokio backend) and switch search_bridge non-asupersync import from tokio::sync::mpsc to crate::runtime_compat::mpsc.","notes":"Completed search_bridge mpsc alias slice. Changes: added unbounded_channel export to runtime_compat::mpsc (tokio backend) in crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs; switched non-asupersync mpsc import in crates/frankenterm-core/src/search_bridge.rs from tokio::sync::mpsc to crate::runtime_compat::mpsc. Verified rustfmt --check passes on both files. Compile validation attempt via rch exec cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --features asupersync-runtime failed remote SSH and fail-opened local; local fallback interrupted immediately.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"HazyRaven","created_at":"2026-02-18T00:27:11.190621Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-18T00:28:34.766441Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T00:28:34.766427Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-2l9kn","title":"Integrate cass (session search) for agent context injection","description":"# Integrate cass for Agent Context Injection

## Skills: /idea-wizard, /extreme-software-optimization

## What cass does
Searches and indexes coding agent session history across 10+ AI tools. Sub-60ms search with Tantivy FTS, BM25 ranking, optional semantic embeddings.

## Current state
Standalone CLI at /Users/jemanuel/projects/coding_agent_session_search.

## Integration plan

### 1. Import cass-core as library
- Use cass search engine directly, not via subprocess
- Share the SQLite/Tantivy index (read-only from FrankenTerm)

### 2. Automatic context injection
When a new agent pane starts work on a task:
- Query cass for related past sessions (by bead ID, file paths, error messages)
- Inject top-3 relevant session excerpts as initial context
- Reduces "cold start" problem where agents re-discover solutions

### 3. On-error search
When FrankenTerm detects an error pattern in pane output:
- Automatically search cass for similar past errors
- If a solution was found before, inject it as a hint
- MCP tool: agent can explicitly search cass

### 4. Swarm learning
- After an agent solves a problem, index the session into cass
- Future agents automatically benefit from collective knowledge
- FrankenTerm orchestrates the index-then-search cycle

### 5. Performance (/extreme-software-optimization)
- Search latency: <100ms for top-10 results
- Index update: background, non-blocking
- Memory: shared index, not per-pane copies

## Tests
- Test context injection: new pane gets relevant history
- Test on-error search: error pattern triggers cass query
- Test search latency under 200-pane load (criterion benchmarks)
- Test index freshness: new session indexed within 60s
- **Test swarm learning cycle**: Agent A solves error → indexed → Agent B hits same error → gets Agent A solution as hint. Verify end-to-end with mock sessions.
- **Test index contention**: 200 panes querying and 10 panes indexing simultaneously, verify no deadlock or degradation

## Acceptance criteria
- cass-core as library dependency
- Automatic context injection on pane start
- On-error pattern search
- MCP tool for explicit search
- Swarm learning: solve→index→search cycle verified end-to-end
- <100ms search latency under concurrent load

## Test Framework Requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks**: Search latency benchmarks:
  - Query latency: benchmark search for varying index sizes (1K, 10K, 100K sessions). Target <60ms p50, <100ms p99.
  - Concurrent search: benchmark 200 concurrent queries against a 100K-session index. Verify no degradation beyond 2x single-query latency.
  - Index write throughput: benchmark indexing new sessions. Target >100 sessions/sec.
  - Context injection: benchmark the full injection pipeline (search + rank + format top-3). Target <200ms end-to-end.
- **Proptest for search result relevance**: Property-based tests for search quality:
  - Generate random session documents with known keywords, search for those keywords, and verify the matching documents appear in the top-10 results
  - Generate pairs of semantically similar sessions (same error message, different context), verify both are returned when searching for the error
  - Generate sessions with no matching content, verify search returns empty results (no false positives from index corruption)
  - For BM25 ranking: generate documents with varying term frequencies, verify ranking order matches BM25 expectations (higher TF-IDF = higher rank)

## Cross-References
- **wa-283h4.11** (LSH error clustering): LSH (Locality-Sensitive Hashing) is used for clustering similar errors in the alien artifact engineering epic. cass's semantic search and LSH error clustering are related search technologies — both find "similar" items in a corpus. Consider sharing the LSH implementation between cass (for session similarity) and the error clustering system (for error similarity). The embedding vectors used by cass could feed directly into LSH buckets.

## Test and Observability Plan
1. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error behavior for logic introduced or changed by this bead.
2. Add deterministic integration and end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise full workflow behavior, including at least one negative/failure scenario and one recovery scenario.
3. Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs (timestamp, subsystem, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions, outcomes, reason_code/error_code).
4. Persist triage artifacts for failed runs (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, and concise failure summaries) so regressions can be root-caused quickly.
5. Ensure test commands and evidence locations are documented in the bead implementation notes and wired into normal validation/CI paths.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deliver full scoped integration with no feature reduction and clear library-grade boundaries to avoid ad-hoc coupling.
2. Require comprehensive validation: unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end scripts across happy paths, edge cases, and failure/rollback behavior.
3. Require structured diagnostics/logging and artifact bundles for decision points, data flows, and failure signatures.
4. Heavy compile/test/lint/benchmark runs must execute via rch and be recorded as evidence.
5. Closure requires auditable command/artifact evidence and residual-risk ownership.","notes":"RECONCILIATION WITH MASTER PLAN: The master plan specifies extending existing cass.rs with a subprocess bridge pattern behind 'cass-export' feature gate, not a full library import. The current bead describes library import + context injection. RECOMMENDATION: Phase 1 = subprocess bridge (extend cass.rs, parse cass CLI JSON output for session search results). Phase 2 = context injection into pane restore flows. This phased approach delivers session search quickly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"StormyWaterfall","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:12:24.731773Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-20T00:25:19.636671Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"comments":[{"id":435,"issue_id":"ft-2l9kn","author":"DarkHarbor","text":"DarkHarbor slice complete (ft-2l9kn): added cass-powered on-error hint injection path in auth recovery workflow.\n\nCode changes (single-file, non-overlapping):\n- crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows.rs\n  - HandleAuthRequired now performs bounded cass lookup on auth events (query from matched_text/extracted text, optional workspace/agent filters, top-3 hints).\n  - Added helper utilities for query normalization, workspace extraction from pane cwd, cass hit formatting, and recovery prompt rendering.\n  - Step 2 now records strategy + cass lookup metadata into audit decision_context and returns StepResult::send_text with a recovery prompt that includes historical cass hints when available.\n  - Added focused unit tests:\n    - handle_auth_required_normalized_cass_query_prefers_matched_text\n    - handle_auth_required_build_recovery_prompt_includes_cass_hints\n\nValidation attempts (all cargo via rch):\n- rch workers probe --all: all 8 workers unreachable (RCH-E100).\n- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-darkharbor-ft2l9kn-check cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib\n  - remote failed -> local fallback -> PASS (Finished dev profile).\n- rch exec -- cargo test targeted for new auth-required tests\n  - remote failed -> local fallback triggered full rebuild; runs were interrupted to avoid prolonged local resource burn.\n\nFormatting:\n- rustfmt --edition 2024 crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows.rs ✅\n\nNet: implementation landed and compile-checked; targeted test execution remains infra-blocked by remote worker outage under strict offload policy.","created_at":"2026-02-20T17:27:53Z"},{"id":1534,"issue_id":"ft-2l9kn","author":"CyanFinch","text":"Implemented session-start cass context injection slice: new workflow handle_session_start_context in workflows.rs (cooldown + cass query candidate generation + top-3 hints + audit action session_start_context + prompt injection), wired claude_code.banner to workflow in patterns.rs, and registered/listed workflow across main.rs runtime/human/robot surfaces. Added targeted workflow unit tests and updated robot workflow list test expectations. Validation blocked for cargo check/clippy/tests due rch worker outage (RCH-E100 across all workers) and rch exec sync race fallback; keeping bead in_progress pending remote offload recovery.","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:54:17Z"},{"id":1536,"issue_id":"ft-2l9kn","author":"CyanFinch","text":"CyanFinch follow-up slice: (1) changed handle_session_start_context to always send the startup context prompt (even when cass returns no hints), while preserving audit result=no_hints for traceability; (2) added tests for generic-context fallback query candidate generation and prompt content when no cass hints are found. Validation: rustfmt applied to workflows.rs. rch workers remain unreachable (all 8 connection_failed/RCH-E100); attempted rch exec cargo test fallbacked to local and was terminated to honor offload-only policy.","created_at":"2026-02-25T21:05:11Z"},{"id":1812,"issue_id":"ft-2l9kn","author":"StormyWaterfall","text":"Implemented the missing session-start cass context injection slice for ft-2l9kn.

Code changes:
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/handlers.rs
  - Added `HandleSessionStartContext` workflow:
    - cooldown guard via audit action `session_start_context`
    - cass query-candidate generation (bead ID/title/cwd/model fallback)
    - top-3 cass hint formatting
    - startup prompt builder that still injects guidance when no hints are found
    - structured audit decision context persisted before injection
  - Added unit tests:
    - handle_session_start_context_metadata
    - handle_session_start_context_handles_session_start_only
    - handle_session_start_context_query_candidates_prefer_bead_id
    - handle_session_start_context_build_prompt_includes_hints
    - handle_session_start_context_build_prompt_no_hints_still_guides
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs
  - Wired `claude_code.banner` to workflow `handle_session_start_context`.
- crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs
  - Registered workflow in watch/human/robot runner setup paths.
  - Added resolve case for manual workflow run.
  - Added workflow to robot workflow-list payload and tests.

Validation attempts (offload-only via rch):
- timeout 45 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-stormy-ft2l9kn-check cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib
  - timed out during remote sync (worker selected, sync did not complete)
- timeout 45 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-stormy-ft2l9kn-test cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib handle_session_start_context_metadata
  - timed out during remote sync
- timeout 45 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-stormy-ft2l9kn-clippy cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --lib -- -D warnings
  - timed out during remote sync
- rustfmt --edition 2024 --check on touched files
  - PASS

Status remains in_progress pending successful remote compile/test evidence once rch worker connectivity recovers.
","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:51:19Z"},{"id":2339,"issue_id":"ft-2l9kn","author":"jemanuel","text":"TanSummit: Added 14 unit tests for HandleSwarmLearningIndex (126b95f9). Tests cover metadata, event filtering, trigger types, supported agents, cooldown config, and workspace extraction. All 14 pass with --no-default-features.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:53:56Z"},{"id":2417,"issue_id":"ft-2l9kn","author":"PurpleOwl","text":"PurpleOwl taking a bounded support slice on ft-2l9kn: refresh remote rch validation for the existing cass workflow surfaces (session-start context, on-error cass search, swarm-learning index, and robot cass CLI) without overlapping prior implementation work. Goal is to surface the current next blocker or record fresh passing evidence on HEAD.","created_at":"2026-03-20T00:11:27Z"},{"id":2418,"issue_id":"ft-2l9kn","author":"PurpleOwl","text":"PurpleOwl validation slice (2026-03-20): refreshed remote offload evidence for the existing cass session-start workflow surface.

Command:
`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-purpleowl-ft-2l9kn CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 RUSTFLAGS=-Cdebuginfo=0 cargo test -j 1 -p frankenterm-core --lib handle_session_start_context -- --nocapture`

Result:
- Worker: `vmi1152480`
- Remote sync succeeded
- Remote test completed successfully in ~13m build time / ~13.0m total
- 6 targeted tests passed, 0 failed:
  - `handle_session_start_context_handles_session_start_only`
  - `handle_session_start_context_metadata`
  - `handle_session_start_context_build_prompt_no_hints_still_guides`
  - `handle_session_start_context_build_prompt_escapes_query_quotes`
  - `handle_session_start_context_build_prompt_includes_hints`
  - `handle_session_start_context_query_candidates_prefer_bead_id`

Takeaway:
- Current HEAD cass session-start context injection path compiles and passes its targeted unit-test slice under `rch`.
- This removes the earlier “workers unreachable / sync timeout” uncertainty for at least one concrete ft-2l9kn validation lane.
- Remaining validation surface still worth refreshing later: `handle_on_error_cass_search`, `handle_swarm_learning_index`, and robot cass CLI parsing/execution paths.
","created_at":"2026-03-20T00:25:19Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-2lp7o","title":"Create Cx capability context threading infrastructure","description":"# Create Cx capability context threading infrastructure

## Background
In asupersync, ALL async effects (spawn, sleep, I/O, trace, checkpoint) are gated through a Cx (capability context) token. Every async function must accept &mut Cx as a parameter. This is fundamentally different from tokio where runtime access is ambient/global.

## What needs to happen
1. Define a project-wide pattern for Cx propagation through the call graph
2. Create a wa-core module (e.g., src/cx.rs or src/context.rs) that wraps and re-exports Cx
3. Establish helper traits/functions that make Cx threading ergonomic
4. Create a RuntimeBuilder wrapper that configures asupersync for FrankenTerm's needs:
   - Worker thread count
   - Poll budget
   - Blocking pool size
   - Presets: current_thread() for tests, multi_thread() for production

## Design considerations
- Cx is &mut (exclusive reference), so it cannot be shared across concurrent tasks without scoping
- Each scope.spawn() gets its own Cx — the runtime manages this
- For existing code that passes Arc<Mutex<T>>, the Cx doesn't replace that — it's orthogonal
- Functions that don't do async effects don't need Cx

## Acceptance criteria
- cx.rs module exists with re-exports and helpers
- RuntimeBuilder configuration works with current_thread() and multi_thread() presets
- A simple integration test can spawn tasks using the new infrastructure
- Documentation comments explain the Cx threading pattern

## Benchmark requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks**: Measure Cx propagation overhead through call chains of depth 1, 5, 10, and 20. Target: zero measurable overhead vs direct function calls (Cx is just a reference pass). Add `benches/cx_propagation.rs` using criterion.

## Property-based testing
- **Proptest**: Property tests for capability access correctness — verify that Cx-gated operations are only accessible when a valid Cx is held, and that capability tokens cannot be forged or duplicated. Use `proptest!` macro with arbitrary capability request sequences.

## Concurrency verification
- **Loom model checking**: Use Loom to verify concurrent Cx access safety — specifically that &mut Cx exclusivity is maintained across scope.spawn() boundaries and that no data races are possible when multiple tasks hold their own Cx references simultaneously.","notes":"Implemented Cx infrastructure: added crates/frankenterm-core/src/cx.rs re-exporting Cx/Budget/Scope + runtime types, CxRuntimeBuilder presets/tuning wrapper, and helper APIs (for_testing/with_cx/with_cx_async/spawn_with_cx/try_spawn_with_cx). Added feature-gated integration tests (tests/asupersync_cx_infrastructure.rs), property tests (tests/proptest_cx_capabilities.rs), and benchmark (benches/cx_propagation.rs) with Cargo bench registration. Validation: cargo check --all-targets passed (workspace); targeted feature checks passed for cx bench/tests via cargo check -p frankenterm-core --features asupersync-runtime --bench cx_propagation --test asupersync_cx_infrastructure --test proptest_cx_capabilities and cargo test ... --test proptest_cx_capabilities (4 passed). Full feature all-targets still fails in unrelated in-progress outcome artifacts (wa-d0m4t) and does not block this bead scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:47:51.253433Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:14:42.247715Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:14:42.247649Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2lp7o","depends_on_id":"ft-hj458","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
